title: asperula × portae
intro: Asperula × portae, a hybrid plant species, is a cross between Asperula tinctoria and Asperula odorata, commonly known as the 'Woodruff'. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Asperula × portae include relief from digestive issues, improved wound healing, and a reduction in symptoms of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Asperula × portae can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often consumed in the form of dried flowers or leaves, which are typically added to salads or used in herbal remedies.
